chemistry.reaction_name: Iridium-catalyzed Synthesis of Saturated N-Heterocycles from Aldehydes and SnAP Reagents
chemistry.product: Saturated N-heterocycles (morpholines, piperazines, thiomorpholines, diazepanes, oxazepanes)
chemistry.scope_summary: Wide variety of saturated N-heterocycles including morpholines, piperazines, thiomorpholines, diazepanes, and oxazepanes from aromatic aldehydes. Compatible with various functional groups including anilines, phenols, esters, nitriles, and halides. N-Boc protected substrates are tolerated.

optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 11 with 2.5 mol % 1, 1:1 CH3CN/MeOH solvent, and 0.25 mL/min flow rate gave 58% yield

figures.caption: Scheme 1. Synthesis of Saturated N-Heterocycles with SnAP and SLAP Reagents

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: oxidation potential of organotin compounds (Eox ~ +1.0 eV for oxygen, Eox ~ +0.7 eV for sulfur)
reduction potential of dialkyaminyl radical (Ered ~ -1.7 eV)
C-Sn bond cleavage occurs via heteroatom oxidation
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Proposed catalytic cycle for copper-promoted SnAP reactions 7
Scheme 2. Substrate Scope of N-Heterocycles from Aldehydes and SnAP Reagents under Continuous Flow Conditions

figures.flow_path: continuous flow through reactor with blue LED irradiation
figures.key_information: 2.5 mol % catalyst 1, 1:1 CH3CN/TFE solvent, room temperature flow conditions with blue LED irradiation
figures.caption: Scheme 3. Gram-Scale Synthesis of Saturated N-Heterocycles with Continuous Flow Photochemistry | Scheme 4. Unsuccessful Cyclizations under Photoredox Conditions

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Lower yield (47% vs 58%) obtained in batch conditions
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Improved yield (58% vs 47%), better heat/mass transfer, and enabling of gram-scale synthesis
translation_logic.what_changed: Flow conditions with continuous flow reactor, flow rate optimization, and external LED irradiation
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photocatalyst (Ir[dF(CF3)ppy]2(dtbbpy)PF6), 2.5 mol% loading, same reaction mechanism
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow enables better control and safer handling of photochemical reactions

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing continuous flow setup with blue LED irradiation
Mechanistic schemes for photoredox catalytic cycle

conflicts: Flow rate discrepancy: optimization table shows 0.25 mL/min but reactor scheme shows 0.2 mL/min - using table value as higher fidelity
notes: Well-documented photoredox flow chemistry paper with clear optimization data and mechanistic insights. Some minor conflicts in flow rates between different sources. Residence time calculated from reactor volume and flow rate.
